decisions.title: Prioritize SCIM reliability fixes over feature development
decisions.context: Multiple SCIM bugs (email provisioning failures, repeated deactivations, admin user blocking) impact enterprise customers. These directly affect 'Embed Deeper' pillar and customer trust.

arguments.for: Enterprise customers expect SSO/SCIM reliability; bugs erode trust and block expansion. Fixing now prevents churn and enables upsell.
arguments.against: Delays Canvas and Explore V2 features that drive new Decisions Captured. May miss Q2 product launches.

decisions.title: Ship Sift Edge GUI table view and enum support in April
decisions.context: Edge GUI improvements (table view ENG-9883, enum/bitfield support ENG-8857) are foundational for 'Build the Moat' by enabling hardware engineers to interact with raw data without Python.

arguments.for: Differentiates Sift from competitors; unblocks hardware teams waiting on Edge features. Directly increases Decisions Captured in embedded systems use cases.
arguments.against: Resources could focus on Canvas or Python Rules which have broader customer base. Edge adoption still nascent.

decisions.title: Consolidate runbooks to internal-docs before FedRAMP audit
decisions.context: Two tickets (ENG-9923, ENG-9297) migrating runbooks to markdown. FedRAMP compliance (ENG-9684) requires documentation standardization. Audit likely in Q2.
decisions.blockedBy: Team capacity split across FedRAMP prep and incident response

arguments.for: FedRAMP certification unlocks federal contracts (Embed Deeper). Documentation debt blocks audit readiness.
arguments.against: Non-customer-facing work. Could defer to Q3 if audit timeline slips.

decisions.title: Resolve annotation visibility bug before customer QBRs
decisions.context: ENG-9861: Annotations missing from Annotation Page but visible in Chart View. Affects Canvas user experience and 'Nail the Core' reliability. Q2 QBRs start April 15.

arguments.for: Annotations are critical for collaborative decision-making (North Star metric). Bug undermines Canvas value prop in customer meetings.
arguments.against: Workaround exists (use Chart View). Other Canvas features (undo/redo, hovercard redesign) may drive more usage.

decisions.title: Defer GUI undo/redo and hovercard redesign to May
decisions.context: ENG-9247 (undo/redo), ENG-9911/9909/9910 (hovercard components) are UX polish. Core Canvas functionality stable. Team capacity constrained by SCIM and Edge work.

arguments.for: Frees engineers for high-impact bugs (annotations, SCIM). Undo/redo nice-to-have, not blocker for Decisions Captured.
arguments.against: Hovercard redesign promised to design partners. Delay risks perception that Canvas development slowing.

decisions.title: Approve RFC for customizable family stat downsampling
decisions.context: ENG-9902/9901: Explore V2 performance optimization for large datasets. Improves 'Nail the Core' by reducing query latency. RFC blocks implementation.

arguments.for: Users report slow family stat queries on 100k+ channels. Downsampling unlocks enterprise-scale usage (Embed Deeper).
arguments.against: Adds complexity to Explore backend. May introduce sampling artifacts that confuse users.

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (80% of time) driven by Q2 planning, FedRAMP prep, and SCIM incident reviews. Only 8 focus hours limits strategic work.
alerts: 3 one-on-ones scheduled same day (April 1) - consider spreading across week
No focus blocks >90min for deep architecture work on Explore V2 performance
FedRAMP compliance review recurring meeting (2hrs weekly) has 40% acceptance rate - cancel or reschedule?
